metadata.scheme_name: Pre Examination Training Centre For All India Service Examinations Like I.A.S., I.P.S., I.R.S., Etc.

raw_data.details: The scheme “Pre Examination Training Centre For All India Service Examinations Like I. A. S., I. P. S., I. R. S., Etc.” introduced on 7th May, 2012 by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, aims to provide coaching and other facilities such as free boarding and lodging to the candidates of the Adi Dravidar and Tribal community, aspiring to qualify for All India Service Examinations (IAS, IPS, IRS, etc.).
raw_data.benefits: Coaching: Free coaching for All India Service Examinations (IAS, IPS, IRS, etc.). Boarding: Free boarding and lodging for candidates. Note: Mess Charges ₹1,200 per month should be paid by the students whose parental income is above ₹1,00,000 per annum.
raw_data.eligibility: The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu. The beneficiary should belong to the Adi Dravidar/Tribal community.
raw_data.application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should collect the application form from the office of the Director, Anna Institute of Management, Chennai-
28.
Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the office of the Director, Anna Institute of Management, Chennai-
28.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
raw_data.documents_required: Proof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority). Income certificate (parental income proof). Educational qualification certificates. Community certificate. Any other documents (if required).

All India Service ExaminationsCoachingLodgingPre Examination TrainingTribal WelfareUPSCCheck EligibilityDetailsThe scheme “Pre Examination Training Centre For All India Service Examinations Like I. A. S., I. P. S., I. R. S., Etc.” introduced on 7th May, 2012 by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, aims to provide coaching and other facilities such as free boarding and lodging to the candidates of the Adi Dravidar and Tribal community, aspiring to qualify for All India Service Examinations (IAS, IPS, IRS, etc.). BenefitsCoaching: Free coaching for All India Service Examinations (IAS, IPS, IRS, etc.). Boarding: Free boarding and lodging for candidates. Note: Mess Charges ₹1,200 per month should be paid by the students whose parental income is above ₹1,00,000 per annum. EligibilityThe beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu. The beneficiary should belong to the Adi Dravidar/Tribal community. Application ProcessOfflineStep 1: The interested applicant should collect the application form from the office of the Director, Anna Institute of Management, Chennai-28. Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the office of the Director, Anna Institute of Management, Chennai-28. Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable). Documents RequiredProof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority). Income certificate (parental income proof). Educational qualification certificates. Community certificate. Any other documents (if required). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the purpose of the scheme? This scheme provides free pre-examination training, boarding, and lodging for Adi Dravidar and Tribal community candidates preparing for All India Service Examinations like IAS, IPS, and IRS.

Who is eligible for this scheme? Adi Dravidar and Tribal community candidates are eligible for the benefits.

What benefits are provided under this scheme? The scheme offers free coaching, along with boarding and lodging.

Are there any costs involved for certain candidates? Yes, candidates whose parental income exceeds ₹1,00,000 per annum are required to pay ₹1,200 per month for mess charges.

What documents are required for applying? Applicants need to provide their caste certificate, income certificate, educational qualification certificates, address proof, and passport-sized photographs.

How can candidates apply for the scheme? Applications can be submitted at the office of the Director, Anna Institute of Management, Chennai.

Is there any age limit for applicants? No, there is no prescribed age limit for the applicants.

How long has this scheme been in place? The scheme was introduced on May 07, 2012.

Is the scheme restricted to certain communities? Yes, the scheme is available exclusively to Adi Dravidar and Tribal community candidates.

Can candidates from outside Tamil Nadu apply? The scheme is primarily intended for residents of Tamil Nadu.

Which examinations are covered under this scheme? The scheme covers training for All India Service Examinations such as IAS, IPS, IRS, and others.

Sources And References[Guidelines](https://www.tn.gov.in/scheme/data_view/26799)OkWas this helpful?
